annotations.title: A way which seemeth just.
annotations.text: <mn>◦</mn> If any Jews, Turks, or Heretics lead a moral good life in this world, it seemeth both to themselves, and to other rude people, that they are in a right way of salvation, but their error in faith leadeth them to eternal damnation.

notes.text: Without true faith none can be saved.

annotations.title: Health of the heart.
annotations.text: As soundness of the heart conserveth the rest of the same body in life, <mn>[1]</mn> so a pure intention often excuseth from mortal sin, as in errors committed of probable, not of gross, nor affected ignorance. <mn>[2]</mn> But secret envy in the heart infecteth and putrefieth man's actions, and destroyeth the work that seemed good: which can no more endure strict examination in the day of judgement, than a rotten cloth can abide washing.
